title: Lemmatization: Dictionary Roots
concept: Lemmatization uses vocabulary and morphological analysis to return a word to its true dictionary root (e.g. 'better' -> 'good'), which is more accurate but slower than stemming. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: N-grams.'

exercises.prompt: What is the main goal of lemmatization?

options.text: Finding the dictionary root of a word
Counting the total letters in a book

exercises.explain: Lemmatization uses a dictionary to find the real base form of a word. For example, it turns 'flies' into 'fly'.

cards.title: Finding the True Root
cards.body: If you search for 'running shoes', you also want to see results for 'ran shoes' or 'run shoes'. How do computers know these words are all secretly the same? They use a smart dictionary helper!

cards.title: What is Lemmatization?
cards.body: Lemmatization is a smart text cleaning tool. Instead of just chopping off word endings, it uses vocabulary and grammar rules to find a word's actual dictionary root (called a 'lemma'). For example, it knows that 'was' comes from 'be'.

cards.title: The Expert Botanist
cards.body: Imagine stemming as a gardener who roughly chops off branches with shears. Lemmatization is like an expert botanist who looks at a leaf, checks their reference book of plants, and tells you exactly which seed it grew from.

cards.title: Roots in Action
cards.body: If we run the words 'saw', 'better', and 'flies' through a lemmatizer, they transform into their dictionary roots: 'see', 'good', and 'fly'. This keeps the original meaning intact!

cards.title: Stemming vs. Lemmatization
cards.body: People often think lemmatization and stemming are the same because they both shorten words. But stemming just cuts off letters blindly (like turning 'generic' into 'gener'), while lemmatization uses a real dictionary to be highly accurate.

cards.title: Comparing the Two
cards.body: Let's compare how stemming and lemmatization handle the word 'better'. One chops blindly, while the other looks it up!

right.text: Looks up the root: 'better' becomes 'good' (keeps the true meaning!)

left.text: Chops off the ending: 'better' becomes 'bet' (which changes the meaning!)

exercises.prompt: Why is lemmatization usually better than stemming?

options.text: Lemmatization just chops off random letters.
Lemmatization is more accurate because it uses a dictionary.

exercises.explain: Stemming uses simple chopping rules, whereas lemmatization analyzes grammar and looks up words in a dictionary database.
Because lemmatization checks a dictionary and analyzes grammar, it requires more computer power and time than simple stemming.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of lemmatization.
exercises.sentence: Lemmatization finds the true root of a word by looking it up in a database or ______.
exercises.explain: Lemmatization uses a dictionary database to ensure the returned word is a real, grammatically correct root word.

exercises.prompt: Which of these outputs shows a lemmatizer at work?
exercises.explain: Sentence B shows the true dictionary root ('good'), which is what a lemmatizer output would look like. Sentence A shows stemming (which cuts letters off blindly), and Sentence C shows simple lowercase cleaning.

exercises.prompt: How does lemmatization find the correct root form of a word like 'worse'?

options.text: It chops off the endings of words using simple rules.
It translates words into different languages.
It looks up words in a dictionary to find their true root.
It randomly replaces letters to shorten words.

exercises.explain: Lemmatization looks up words in a real dictionary to find their true base form. Next up: N-grams.
